coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of web accessibility and its purpose

Ability to run automated accessibility checks under supervision
Elementary knowledge of assistive technologies such as screen readers
Support in documenting accessibility issues found in a product
Understanding of semantic HTML and its role in accessibility
Ability to check colour contrast against WCAG thresholds
Awareness of keyboard navigation requirements

Basic knowledge of ARIA roles and attributes
Understanding of accessibility in design tools such as Figma
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Assists in documenting accessibility issues
Is learning accessibility fundamentals

coreCompetencies: Execution of accessibility audits against WCAG 2.2 success criteria
Manual testing with screen readers such as NVDA or VoiceOver
Verification of keyboard operability and focus management
Documentation of findings with severity and remediation guidance

Support in reviewing designs for accessibility before build
Testing of colour contrast and text resizing requirements
Collaboration with developers to fix accessibility defects
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with the EN 301 549 standard
Knowledge of accessibility testing in the browser developer tools
Understanding of responsive and zoom accessibility
indicators: Works with regular supervision
Runs accessibility audits for a component
Contributes remediation guidance to developers
